input:
They went out of the built-up area of Makkah to pray where they could not be seen. Sometimes they were discovered by non-Muslims, as indeed happened one day with the Prophet who was offering his prayers with his young cousin ʿAlī. Abū Ṭālib was passing by and it was the first time he saw Islamic prayer. He asked his nephew, the Prophet, about his prayer and asked ʿAlī why he was joining him. ʿAlī told him that he had accepted the faith of Islam and that he believed in Muhammad as a Prophet and a messenger of God.
Source: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 9: Misrepresentation

output.source_reference: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Chapter 9: Misrepresentation

characters.name: Abu Talib al-Muttalib
characters.appearance: Mid 50s, medium build, dignified posture. Darker olive skin, thick grey-streaked beard with some dark hairs, neatly kept. Wearing plain brown woolen cloak, white cloth wrapped as loose head covering. Worn leather sandals. Strong features, stern expression.

characters.name: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
characters.appearance: Early 50s, medium build, dignified upright posture. Wearing a simple white thobe with a green cloak draped over shoulders, white turban with tail between shoulder blades. Simple leather sandals. Always shown from behind, face never visible. Distinguished by green cloak and subtle warm light.

characters.name: Ali ibn Abi Talib
characters.appearance: Late teens, slender build, energetic posture. Lighter olive skin, dark hair, small beard just starting. Wearing off-white cotton thobe, plain brown woolen cloak, simple white cloth wrapped as head covering. Worn leather sandals. Wide-eyed, enthusiastic expression.

output.moral_lesson: Accepting faith is a personal decision, and one should be prepared to explain and justify it to others.
output.narrative_text: One day, Abu Talib saw the Prophet praying with his young cousin Ali. He had never seen such a prayer before and asked the Prophet about it. Ali told Abu Talib that he had accepted Islam and believed in the Prophet as a messenger of God.

compliance.notes: Prophet shown from behind, Ali shown with full face. Abu Talib shown with full face, questioning expression. Modest clothing for all characters. Warm, respectful scene, suitable for children.

output.image_prompt: Children's watercolor illustration. Undeveloped area outside of 7th century Makkah, simple mud-brick walls in the background. The Prophet, a dignified man in white thobe and green cloak, white turban, seen entirely from behind, with his head bowed in prayer. Nearby, his young cousin Ali, a slender teenage Arab boy with darker olive skin, off-white thobe and brown cloak, simple white cloth wrapped as head covering, kneeling facing the Prophet, hands clasped in prayer. Standing behind them, Abu Talib, a strong-built Arab man with darker olive skin, thick grey-streaked beard, brown woolen cloak, white cloth wrapped as head covering, looking at them with a question on his face. Soft desert landscape, gentle blue sky with few clouds. Medium shot, eye level. Warm, muted palette. No modern objects.
